claim: Sending EU citizen data to US-hosted APIs (even embedding APIs in Virginia) violates GDPR and data sovereignty
actionable: For EU data: verify all APIs (including embeddings, LLMs, storage) run in EU infrastructure; use on-premise solutions for PII.
source_title: What Breaks When You Build AI Under Sovereignty Constraints

claim: Sovereignty requires four pillars: data (storage/processing location), model (control/origin), infrastructure (compute location), operations (auditability)
actionable: When designing sovereign AI systems, audit each pillar: Can you swap models? Do you have reproducible logs? Can you respond to incidents without vendor help?
source_title: What Breaks When You Build AI Under Sovereignty Constraints

claim: Retrofitting sovereignty breaks existing systems: model swaps require prompt/architecture changes, data migration creates search complexity, on-prem reveals hidden vendor lock-in
actionable: Design for sovereignty from inception: use model-agnostic orchestration (Haystack), separate prompts from logic, abstract storage layers.
source_title: What Breaks When You Build AI Under Sovereignty Constraints

claim: Orchestration frameworks with typed inputs/outputs and explicit data flow (like Haystack) enable swappable components and auditability
actionable: Use frameworks with: (1) typed component interfaces, (2) YAML-serializable pipelines, (3) explicit data lineage tracing for compliance.
source_title: What Breaks When You Build AI Under Sovereignty Constraints

claim: High-stakes domains (finance, healthcare, HR) need fully air-gapped solutions; other enterprises can operate at partial sovereignty
actionable: Assess risk level of use case; if high-stakes, pursue air-gapped infrastructure; otherwise, optimize for specific sovereignty pillars.
source_title: What Breaks When You Build AI Under Sovereignty Constraints

claim: Multi-database search becomes a bottleneck when moving data across jurisdictions; decide whether to query all DBs or classify first
actionable: For multi-jurisdiction data, design query strategy upfront: (1) query classification→single DB lookup, or (2) broadcast to all DBs and deduplicate.
source_title: What Breaks When You Build AI Under Sovereignty Constraints
